content.problemStatement:
You are given a string TTT and nnn (not necessarily distinct) strings S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SnS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_nS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Sn‚Äã. What is the maximum length of a substring of TTT that can be formed by choosing some strings from S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SnS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_nS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Sn‚Äã, and concatenating them in some non decreasing order of length. Please note that there might be multiple non decreasing orders possible.

Formally,

Consider all the strings YYY that can be obtained as follows:

Initialize YYY as empty string, L=0L = 0L=0, X={1,2,‚Ä¶,n}X = \{1, 2, \ldots, n\}X={1,2,‚Ä¶,n}. Choose 0‚â§t‚â§n0 \leq t \leq n0‚â§t‚â§n.

Repeat the following procedure ttt times:


Choose an index i‚ààXi \in Xi‚ààX such that ‚à£Si‚à£‚â•L|S_i| \geq L‚à£Si‚Äã‚à£‚â•L.
Add SiS_iSi‚Äã to the end of YYY.
Replace LLL by ‚à£Si‚à£|S_i|‚à£Si‚Äã‚à£
Remove iii from XXX.
What is the length of the longest substring of TTT that can be formed in this way?

###Input:


First line will contain QQQ, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each testcase contains the string TTT.
The second line of each testcase contains nnn.
iii-th of the next nnn lines contain the string SiS_iSi‚Äã
###Output:
For each testcase, output the length of the longest substring of TTT that can be formed in the way described. If there is no non empty substring of TTT that can be formed, print 0.

###Constraints


1‚â§Q‚â§1041 \leq Q \leq 10^41‚â§Q‚â§104
1‚â§n,‚à£Si‚à£1 \leq n, |S_i|1‚â§n,‚à£Si‚Äã‚à£
All strings in the input consist of lowercase english letters only.
The sum of lengths of all the strings in the input doesn't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first testcase, we can choose the strings a and bcd and concatenate to get the string abcd.
In the second testcase, you can choose all the strings and concatenate them in the order a, bc, de and fg to get abcdefg.
In the third testcase choose both the strings a and a to get aa. Note that one string can be used only once, and hence we can't form aaa.
